name: Notion
description: Notion's cloud workspace for documents and knowledge management; yields workspace security-setting evidence, member and guest reports, the Enterprise-plan audit log and SCIM-based membership listings.

steps.action: In the Notion sidebar select Settings, open the Security tab and take dated screenshots of the sharing controls — 'Disable guests', 'Disable publishing sites, forms and public links', 'Disable export' and 'Disable moving or duplicating pages to other workspaces'
steps.audit_note: Several of these toggles are plan-gated (the full set requires Business or Enterprise); note the workspace plan in the evidence record, and treat the dated screenshot as the primary record because these settings have no export

steps.action: Go to Settings > People and screenshot the Members tab (each person's role: Workspace owner, Membership admin or Member) and the Guests tab, which lists every guest together with the pages they can access
steps.audit_note: The Guests tab is the only complete inventory of external collaborators — the public API does not return guests, so capture it manually

steps.action: On the Enterprise plan, go to Settings > Audit log, filter by date, person or event type, and select Export to download the filtered events as CSV (the log shows up to 365 days of history); on Free, Plus or Business plans there is no audit log, so record that limitation against the evidence ID
steps.audit_note: Exports include events up to 2 hours before the export time; the 365-day lookback means a weekly capture cadence comfortably avoids gaps

steps.action: For policy review cadence evidence, open each in-scope policy page, select More actions (•••) > Version history and screenshot the edit history showing editors and dates
steps.permissions_required: Full access to the policy pages
steps.audit_note: Version history retention is plan-gated — 7 days on Free, 30 on Plus, 90 on Business and unlimited on Enterprise — so screenshots must be captured within the retention window on lower plans

steps.action: Upload the screenshots and CSV exports to the evidence repository with the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Ensure no page content containing sensitive data is visible in screenshots, and restrict access to exports containing member email addresses

L2.title: Notion Recurring Export Cadence
L2.estimated_time: 45 minutes initial setup, then 15 minutes per run

steps.action: Notion has no natively scheduled report delivery, so anchor the cadence on a recurring monthly task (a recurring Notion database entry works well) and record each run against the evidence ID
steps.audit_note: Because delivery is not automated, the completed recurring task itself is the evidence that the cadence operated

steps.action: On the Enterprise plan, go to Settings > Audit log each month, filter to the previous calendar month and select Export to download the CSV
steps.audit_note: The 365-day lookback means monthly exports build a continuous archive well before the window closes

steps.action: On the Enterprise plan, go to Settings > Analytics, open the Members tab and export the member activity data to CSV for the same period
steps.audit_note: Regular exports preserve historical activity data that supports leaver checks and dormant-account reviews

steps.action: On Free, Plus or Business plans, substitute dated screenshots of Settings > Security and Settings > People (Members and Guests tabs) at the same monthly cadence, and note the plan limitation in the evidence record
File each export in the evidence repository with restricted access
steps.security_note: Audit log and analytics exports contain member email addresses and activity detail; limit access to the compliance team

steps.action: Create an internal integration at www.notion.so/profile/integrations with read-only content capabilities plus the user information capability, and have a workspace owner approve and connect it to the workspace
steps.permissions_required: Workspace owner (to create and connect the integration)
steps.security_note: Store the integration secret in a secrets manager — grant only the read capabilities the collector needs, and do not reuse the secret across environments

steps.action: Run a daily member inventory: GET https://api.notion.com/v1/users with a Bearer token and Notion-Version header, following start_cursor/next_cursor pagination; note honestly that this returns members and bots only — guests are excluded, so it cannot replace the guest review
steps.audit_note: Because guests never appear in /v1/users, pair this pull with the manual Guests tab capture or SCIM to keep external-access evidence complete

steps.action: On the Enterprise plan, generate a SCIM token at Settings > Identity & provisioning > SCIM configuration, then pull authoritative membership daily: GET https://api.notion.com/scim/v2/Users and GET https://api.notion.com/scim/v2/Groups with Bearer authentication
steps.security_note: SCIM tokens are tied to the workspace owner who generated them and are revoked if that owner leaves or is downgraded — generate the token from a service account owner and store it in a secrets manager

steps.action: Record the audit gap honestly: the public Notion API exposes no audit log endpoint, so programmatic audit event collection is only possible via the Enterprise SIEM stream (see L4) or the manual CSV export — keep the monthly export task running alongside the API pulls
steps.audit_note: Documenting a known collection gap and its compensating control is stronger evidence than silently omitting it

steps.action: Add error handling and validation: respect the API rate limit (an average of three requests per second), follow next_cursor pagination to completion, alert on 401/403 responses or empty result sets, and store outputs with run timestamps and checksums
steps.audit_note: Recording run metadata and checksums makes each collected file independently verifiable

steps.action: On the Enterprise plan, stream audit events continuously: go to Settings > Connections, select Custom SIEM integration, enter your collector's webhook URL and select Connect — all audit log events are then delivered in real time as JSON, with managed connectors also available for Splunk, Sumo Logic, Panther and Datadog
steps.security_note: Use an HTTPS endpoint, restrict who can read the stream at the collector, and treat the payloads as sensitive — they include member identities and page activity
steps.audit_note: The SIEM stream is Enterprise-only; on lower plans there is no continuous audit feed, so the closest real alternative is the L2/L3 export cadence

steps.action: Map the ingested stream against Notion's published audit log event catalogue (page, workspace, account, teamspace, form and data source events) and confirm the event types your controls rely on actually arrive

Reconcile membership daily: compare SCIM Users/Groups pulls against member-added and member-removed events in the audit stream, and alert on discrepancies such as members present in SCIM but missing join events
Measure completeness: reconcile SIEM-received event counts against a monthly audit log CSV export for the same window, and alert on gaps or webhook delivery failures
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics from reconciliation are what turn continuous collection into audit-grade evidence

steps.action: Keep the human checks that automation cannot cover: workspace security settings and the guest inventory have no API, so retain a scheduled review of Settings > Security and the Guests tab and track it as a recurring control task
Quarterly, rotate the integration secret and SCIM token, review the workspace owner list, and publish a weekly dashboard covering guest count trend, publicly published pages and audit stream health
steps.security_note: SCIM tokens survive until revoked and are invalidated when their owner leaves — rotation tied to the quarterly access review prevents both stale and silently dead credentials
